Crypto Scams

Crypto Investment Scam Checklist: Fast Tests Before You Deposit

High returns, social proof, and smooth onboarding are common features of crypto fraud.

Published 2026-04-06 · Beat the Scam Editorial Team · 1 min read

crypto scaminvestment scam cryptofake trading platform
Key rule: verify through an official route you opened yourself, not the link, number, app, or payment details supplied by the suspicious message.
On this page
  1. Separate technology from the offer
  2. Check whether withdrawals are possible
  3. Beware managed account promises
  4. Question recovery services
  5. Verify the entity, not the chat
  6. Use a small test only if you fully accept the risk

Separate technology from the offer

A scam can mention real coins, real exchanges, or real blockchain terms and still be fraudulent. Your job is to assess the company, custody arrangement, and withdrawal process, not just the buzzwords.

Check whether withdrawals are possible

Many fake platforms accept deposits easily but invent taxes, unlocking fees, or verification costs when you try to withdraw. Search specifically for complaints about blocked withdrawals rather than general reviews.

Beware managed account promises

If someone on social media, WhatsApp, Telegram, or a dating app offers to trade on your behalf, assume elevated risk immediately. Unsolicited managed returns are a common route into crypto fraud.

Question recovery services

A second scam often follows the first. Fraudsters contact victims pretending to be recovery experts, regulators, or forensic specialists who can get funds back for an upfront fee.

Verify the entity, not the chat

Check who owns the website, where the company claims to operate, whether regulatory claims are real, and whether there is evidence of genuine trading infrastructure rather than screenshots and testimonials.

Use a small test only if you fully accept the risk

A scam may allow one small withdrawal to build confidence before encouraging a larger deposit. A successful test does not prove the platform is safe.

Frequently asked questions

Can a scam platform show fake profits?

Yes. The dashboard can be entirely fabricated.

Is celebrity endorsement proof?

No. Fake endorsements and edited videos are common in crypto fraud.

Should I pay a tax or release fee to withdraw?

Treat that as a major red flag and verify independently before sending anything.

Think you’ve spotted a scam? Use the AI scam checker for an instant analysis, or report it to Action Fraud.

Fast checks

  • Pause before sending money or credentials
  • Verify with an official site, app, or number
  • Never share one-time passcodes
  • Be sceptical of bank transfer pressure
